$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for IDE support

environmentUrl.description: The target Dynatrace environment URL where the app will be deployed. Must be a valid HTTPS URL pointing to your Dynatrace tenant.
environmentUrl.errorMessage: must contain a valid 'environmentUrl'. Please specify one in your 'app.config.(json|js|ts|cts)' file

build.description: Configuration options for the build process including source paths, output settings, bundling behavior, and asset management. Controls how your app is compiled and packaged.

app.description: Core application metadata and configuration including app ID, name, version, description, permissions, intents, actions, and other manifest properties required for app registration.

injectSdk.description: Controls whether the Dynatrace SDK is automatically injected into your application. When enabled, provides access to Dynatrace platform APIs and services.

server.description: Development server configuration including port, host, SSL settings, and CSP options. Used when running the app locally during development.

plugins.description: ESBuild plugins for customizing the build process

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

CliOptions.description: Main configuration object for the dt-app toolkit. The toolkit automatically resolves a config file named `app.config.json` from the project's root directory when running commands. This type defines all available configuration options for building, serving, and managing Dynatrace apps.

required.environmentUrl: must contain a valid 'environmentUrl'. Please specify one in your 'app.config.(json|js|ts|cts)' file

index.description: Path to the main HTML entry point file, relative to the project root. This file serves as the template for the generated application.

mode.description: Build environment mode affecting optimization, minification, and debugging features. Production mode enables optimizations while development mode preserves debugging info.
mode.default: 'production' when building and 'development' when using development server

sourceMaps.description:
Controls source map generation for debugging support.
- `true`: Generate source maps for application code only
- `'all'`: Include source maps for both application code and node_modules
- `false`: No source maps generated
sourceMaps.default: undefined (treated as 'all' for development, 'false' for production builds)
sourceMaps.errorMessage: must be one of the following values: true, false, 'all'

sourceRoot.description: All source file paths are resolved relative to this directory. <sourceRoot>/{ui,api,actions,settings,documents} Is relative to project root.

baseHref.description: Base path for application assets in the final build output. Used to configure routing and asset loading in the deployed application.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

plugins.description: ESBuild plugins for customizing the build process

glob.description: Glob pattern specifying which files to include from the input directory. Use UNIX-style path separators ('/') regardless of operating system. Supports standard glob syntax like wildcards (*) and directory recursion (**).
glob.errorMessage: has to be a glob pattern

errorMessage.format: has to be a glob pattern

ignore.description: Array of glob patterns for files to exclude from the asset bundle. Useful for filtering out development files, tests, or temporary resources.

input.description: Source directory containing the assets to be processed. Can be relative to project root or an absolute file system path. All glob patterns are executed within this directory.

output.description: Destination directory where assets will be copied in the build output. Path is relative to the build distribution directory (distDir). Maintains directory structure from the input folder.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

items.description: Configuration for static assets that are bundled with the application. Assets are served by the development server and included in the deployed app package. Supports file patterns, exclusions, and custom output paths for flexible asset management.

required.glob: has to be a glob pattern

assets.description: Static asset configurations defining how files are copied and served. Each asset configuration handles different types of static resources.

ui.description: UI-specific build configuration including asset management and plugin settings. Controls how frontend resources are processed and bundled.

plugins.description: ESBuild plugins for customizing the build process

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

api.description: API-specific build configuration for backend processing and plugin integration. Manages server-side code compilation and plugin execution.

addOrOverride.description: Override specific Dynatrace dependencies with custom versions. Key is the dependency name, value is the semver-compliant version string.

ignore.description: List of Dynatrace dependency names to exclude from the build.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

dynatraceDependencies.description: Advanced configuration for modifying Dynatrace platform dependencies. Allows overriding or ignoring specific platform dependencies during build.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

AppBuildOptions.description: Configuration options for customizing the application build process. Controls compilation, bundling, asset processing, and output generation.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it
contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

transformSync.$comment: (input: string | Uint8Array, options?: SameShape<TransformOptions, T>) => TransformResult<T>

id.description: Globally unique application identifier used for registration and deployment. Must be alphanumeric lowercase string, maximum 50 characters. Follow reverse domain notation (e.g., 'com.company.app').

name.description: Human-readable application name displayed in the Dynatrace interface. Maximum 40 characters, should be descriptive and user-friendly.

version.description: Semantic version string following semver specification (e.g., '1.0.0'). Used for app lifecycle management and compatibility checks.

description.description: Brief application description explaining its purpose and functionality. Maximum 80 characters, displayed in app catalogs and listings.

name.description: Human-readable name for the document type displayed in the Dynatrace interface. Used in file browsers, document listings, and type selection dialogs.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

documentTypes.description: Document type definitions for apps that handle custom document formats. Defines how the app processes and displays specific document types. Is relative to the sourceRoot.

icon.description: Path to the application icon file, relative to the project root. Supports SVG (recommended) or PNG formats. Auto-generated if not specified. Icon appears in app launchers and navigation interfaces.

hidden.description: Controls visibility in the Dynatrace App Launcher. Set to true for widget-only apps or background services that don't need launcher presence.

intents.description: Application capabilities for handling system intents and cross-app communication. Defines what types of data and actions the app can process from other apps.

pageTokens.description: Named page routes exposed by the app for deep linking and navigation. Maps logical page names to URL paths within the application.

actions.description: Workflow automation actions provided by the app. Enables integration with Dynatrace automation workflows and external systems. Is relative to the sourceRoot.

csp.description: Content Security Policy directives specific to this application. Defines allowed sources for fonts, images, scripts, and other resources.

functionSandbox.description: Defines special sandbox options that enable additional capabilities for functions

name.description: The OAuth scope identifier as defined by Dynatrace API documentation. Corresponds to specific API endpoints or data access permissions.

comment.description: Explanation of why this scope is required by the application. Used for permission reviews and compliance documentation.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

items.description: OAuth 2.0 scope definition for Dynatrace API access permissions. Defines specific API capabilities required by the application with justification.

scopes.description: OAuth scopes required by the application for accessing Dynatrace APIs and data. Each scope must include a descriptive comment explaining its necessity.

selfMonitoringAgent.description: URL endpoint for the self-monitoring agent script. Enables application performance monitoring and diagnostics within Dynatrace.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

uiCommands.description: UI command definitions for extending Dynatrace interface functionality. Allows apps to contribute commands to menus, toolbars, and context actions.

name.description: Display name for the settings widget shown in the configuration interface.

description.description: Optional description explaining the purpose and impact of these settings.

schemaIds.description: Array of JSON schema identifiers that define the structure and validation rules for the configuration data managed by this settings widget.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

settings.description: Configuration widgets for app-specific settings management. Provides structured forms for users to configure app behavior and preferences. Is relative to the sourceRoot.

name.description: Optional display name for the document handler in the Dynatrace interface.

description.description: Optional description of the document type and its processing capabilities.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

documents.description: Documents handling configuration for apps that process custom document types. Defines document schemas, rendering, and interaction capabilities. Is relative to the sourceRoot.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

AppOptions.description: Core application metadata and configuration options used to generate the Dynatrace app manifest. Defines the app's identity, capabilities, security requirements, and integration features.

title.description: Human-readable title displayed in workflow editors and action catalogs. Should be concise and descriptive of the action's primary function.

description.description: Detailed description explaining the action's purpose, inputs, and behavior. Used in documentation and workflow design interfaces.

name.description: Unique identifier for the action within the application scope. Used for action registration, invocation, and workflow references.

stateful.description: Legacy flag indicating whether the action maintains state between executions.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

plugins.description: ESBuild plugins for customizing the build process

entryPoint.description: Main TypeScript/React entry file for the action's user interface. Path is relative to the action's directory (`/widgets/actions/<name>`). This file typically exports the action's main React component.

tsconfig.description: TypeScript configuration file for compiling the action's code. Path is relative to the action's directory (`/widgets/actions/<name>`). Should contain action-specific compiler options and path mappings.

build.description: Build configuration specific to this action's UI components and compilation.

font-src.description: Specifies valid sources for web fonts loaded by the application.

img-src.description: Defines allowed sources for images and favicons used in the application. Includes img elements, CSS background images, and favicon resources.

media-src.description: Controls valid sources for audio and video media content. Applies to HTML5 audio/video elements and media streaming resources.

style-src.description: Specifies trusted sources for CSS stylesheets and style resources. Covers external stylesheets, inline styles, and CSS imports.

script-src.description: Defines allowed sources for JavaScript code execution and script resources. Includes external scripts, inline event handlers, eval(), and XSLT transformations. Critical for preventing XSS attacks through script injection.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

CSPAppDirectives.description: Content Security Policy (CSP) directives configuration for enhanced application security. Defines trusted sources for various types of resources to prevent XSS and data injection attacks. Each directive type corresponds to a specific CSP header directive.

value.description: The CSP directive value (e.g., URL, keyword like 'self', 'unsafe-inline'). Must follow CSP specification syntax for the corresponding directive type.

comment.description: Human-readable explanation for why this directive value is necessary. Required for security audits and compliance documentation.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

DirectiveValue.description: Individual CSP directive value with documentation for security compliance. Combines the actual directive value with explanatory context for security audits.

unsafe-eval.description: Explicitly allows the app to use JavaScript features that evaluate code from strings at runtime (like eval()). This opens up the risk of remote-code execution (RCE) from untrusted input.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

FunctionSandbox.description: Defines special sandbox options that enable additional capabilities for functions

value.description: Enables/disables the usage of the eval() JavaScript function

comment.description: Additional comment to specify why the eval() function is being used

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

UnsafeEval.description: Explicitly allows the app to use JavaScript features that evaluate code from strings at runtime (like eval()). This opens up the risk of remote-code execution (RCE) from untrusted input.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

keyBindings.description: When not given, command is Informational only

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it
contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

port.description: TCP port number for the development server to listen on. Choose an available port that doesn't conflict with other services.

host.description: Network interface IP address to bind the development server. Use '127.0.0.1' for localhost only

showWarnings.description: Controls visibility of build warnings in the development server console output. Useful for debugging build issues during development.

enableCSP.description: Enables Content Security Policy (CSP) headers for enhanced security during development. When enabled, generates default CSP directives and merges with any custom directives. Frame-related directives are set to allow all sources in local development.

https.description: SSL/TLS certificate configuration for HTTPS development server. Provides secure local development environment with custom certificates.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

ServerOptions.description: Configuration options for the local development server. Controls how the application is served during development including network settings, security policies, and SSL configuration.

cert.description: File system path to the SSL certificate in PEM format.

key.description: File system path to the private key file in PEM format.

additionalProperties.errorMessage: contains unknown property ${0#}. Please check if there is a typo or remove it

ServerHttpsOptions.description: HTTPS configuration options for the development server.
